import atexit
import json
import multiprocessing
import time
import warnings
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Union

import aiohttp
import requests

from sglang.global_config import global_config
from sglang.lang.backend.base_backend import BaseBackend
from sglang.lang.chat_template import get_chat_template, get_chat_template_by_model_path
from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesDecision, ChoicesSamplingMethod
from sglang.lang.interpreter import StreamExecutor
from sglang.lang.ir import (
    REGEX_BOOL,
    REGEX_FLOAT,
    REGEX_INT,
    REGEX_STR,
    SglSamplingParams,
)
from sglang.utils import http_request


class RuntimeEndpoint(BaseBackend):
    def __init__(
        self,
        base_url: str,
        api_key: Optional[str] = None,
        verify: Optional[str] = None,
        chat_template_name: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.support_concate_and_append = True

        self.base_url = base_url
        self.api_key = api_key
        self.verify = verify

        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/get_model_info",
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)
        self.model_info = res.json()

        if chat_template_name:
            self.chat_template = get_chat_template(chat_template_name)
        else:
            self.chat_template = get_chat_template_by_model_path(
                self.model_info["model_path"]
            )

    def get_model_name(self):
        return self.model_info["model_path"]

    def flush_cache(self):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/flush_cache",
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
            method="POST",
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def get_server_info(self):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/get_server_info",
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)
        return res.json()

    def get_chat_template(self):
        return self.chat_template

    def cache_prefix(self, prefix_str: str):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/generate",
            json={"text": prefix_str, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}},
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def start_profile(self):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/start_profile",
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def stop_profile(self):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/stop_profile",
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def commit_lazy_operations(self, s: StreamExecutor):
        data = {"text": s.text_, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}}
        self._add_images(s, data)
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/generate",
            json=data,
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def fill_image(self, s: StreamExecutor):
        data = {"text": s.text_, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}}
        self._add_images(s, data)
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/generate",
            json=data,
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def _handle_dtype_to_regex(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
        if sampling_params.dtype is None:
            return

        if sampling_params.stop == ():
            sampling_params.stop = []

        dtype_regex = None
        if sampling_params.dtype in ["int", int]:

            dtype_regex = REGEX_INT
            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["float", float]:

            dtype_regex = REGEX_FLOAT
            sampling_params.stop.extend([" ", "\n"])
        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["str", str]:

            dtype_regex = REGEX_STR
        elif sampling_params.dtype in ["bool", bool]:

            dtype_regex = REGEX_BOOL
        else:
            raise RuntimeError(f"Invalid dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}")

        if dtype_regex is not None and sampling_params.regex is not None:
            warnings.warn(
                f"Both dtype and regex are set. Only dtype will be used. dtype: {sampling_params.dtype}, regex: {sampling_params.regex}"
            )

        sampling_params.regex = dtype_regex

    def generate(
        self,
        s: StreamExecutor,
        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
    ):
        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
        data = {
            "text": s.text_,
            "sampling_params": {
                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
            },
        }

        for item in [
            "return_logprob",
            "logprob_start_len",
            "top_logprobs_num",
            "return_text_in_logprobs",
        ]:
            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
            if value is not None:
                data[item] = value

        self._add_images(s, data)

        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/generate",
            json=data,
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

        obj = res.json()
        comp = obj["text"]
        return comp, obj["meta_info"]

    def generate_stream(
        self,
        s: StreamExecutor,
        sampling_params: SglSamplingParams,
    ):
        self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)

        data = {
            "text": s.text_,
            "sampling_params": {
                "skip_special_tokens": global_config.skip_special_tokens_in_output,
                "spaces_between_special_tokens": global_config.spaces_between_special_tokens_in_out,
                **sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs(),
            },
        }

        for item in [
            "return_logprob",
            "logprob_start_len",
            "top_logprobs_num",
            "return_text_in_logprobs",
        ]:
            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
            if value is not None:
                data[item] = value

        data["stream"] = True
        self._add_images(s, data)

        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/generate",
            json=data,
            stream=True,
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)
        pos = 0

        for chunk in res.iter_lines(decode_unicode=False):
            chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
            if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):
                if chunk == "data: [DONE]":
                    break
                data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
                chunk_text = data["text"][pos:]
                meta_info = data["meta_info"]
                pos += len(chunk_text)
                yield chunk_text, meta_info

    def select(
        self,
        s: StreamExecutor,
        choices: List[str],
        temperature: float,
        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
        assert temperature <= 1e-5

        # Cache common prefix
        data = {"text": s.text_, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}}
        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
        prompt_len = obj["meta_info"]["prompt_tokens"]
        logprob_start_len = max(prompt_len - 2, 0)  # For token healing

        # Compute logprob
        data = {
            "text": [s.text_ + c for c in choices],
            "sampling_params": {
                "max_new_tokens": 0,
                "temperature": 0,
            },
            "return_logprob": True,
            "return_text_in_logprobs": True,
            "logprob_start_len": logprob_start_len,
        }
        obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)

        input_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
        output_token_logprobs = [r["meta_info"]["output_token_logprobs"] for r in obj]
        normalized_prompt_logprobs = [
            compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"])
            for r in obj
        ]

        # Remove extra token if no token healing occurred
        for i in range(len(input_token_logprobs)):
            healed_token_str = input_token_logprobs[i][0][-1]
            if s.text_.endswith(healed_token_str):
                healed_token_logprob = input_token_logprobs[i][0][0]
                normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] = (
                    normalized_prompt_logprobs[i] * len(input_token_logprobs[i])
                    - healed_token_logprob
                ) / (len(input_token_logprobs[i]) - 1)
                input_token_logprobs[i] = input_token_logprobs[i][1:]

        # Compute unconditional logprobs if required
        if choices_method.requires_unconditional_logprobs:
            input_ids = [[el[1] for el in subl] for subl in input_token_logprobs]
            data = {
                "input_ids": input_ids,
                "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0},
                "return_logprob": True,
            }
            obj = self._generate_http_request(s, data)
            unconditional_token_logprobs = [
                r["meta_info"]["input_token_logprobs"] for r in obj
            ]
        else:
            unconditional_token_logprobs = None

        return choices_method(
            choices=choices,
            normalized_prompt_logprobs=normalized_prompt_logprobs,
            input_token_logprobs=input_token_logprobs,
            output_token_logprobs=output_token_logprobs,
            unconditional_token_logprobs=unconditional_token_logprobs,
        )

    def concatenate_and_append(self, src_rids: List[str], dst_rid: str):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/concate_and_append_request",
            json={"src_rids": src_rids, "dst_rid": dst_rid},
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def _generate_http_request(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):
        self._add_images(s, data)
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/generate",
            json=data,
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)
        return res.json()

    def _add_images(self, s: StreamExecutor, data):
        if s.images_:
            assert len(s.images_) == 1, "Only support one image."
            data["image_data"] = s.images_[0][1]

    def _assert_success(self, res):
        if res.status_code != 200:
            try:
                content = res.json()
            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                content = res.text
            raise RuntimeError(content)


def compute_normalized_prompt_logprobs(input_logprobs):
    values = [x[0] for x in input_logprobs if x[0]]
    return sum(values) / len(values)


class Runtime:
    """
    A wrapper for the HTTP server.
    This is used for launching the server in a python program without
    using the command line interface.

    It is mainly used for the frontend language.
    You should use the Engine class if you want to do normal offline processing without the frontend language.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        log_level: str = "error",
        launch_timeout: float = 300.0,
        *args,
        **kwargs,
    ):
        """See the arguments in server_args.py::ServerArgs

        Args:
            log_level: Log level for the server.
            timeout: Timeout in seconds for waiting for the server to start.
            *args: Additional arguments passed to ServerArgs.
            **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments passed to ServerArgs.
        """
        # We delay the import of any `sglang.srt` components in `sglang.lang`, so users can run
        # client code without installing SRT server and its dependency if they want.
        from sglang.srt.entrypoints.http_server import launch_server
        from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
        from sglang.srt.utils import is_port_available

        self.server_args = ServerArgs(*args, log_level=log_level, **kwargs)

        # Pre-allocate ports
        for port in range(self.server_args.port, 40000):
            if is_port_available(port):
                break
        self.server_args.port = port

        self.url = self.server_args.url()
        self.generate_url = self.url + "/generate"

        # NOTE: We store pid instead of proc to fix some issues during __delete__
        self.pid = None

        ctx = multiprocessing.get_context("spawn")
        proc = ctx.Process(
            target=launch_server,
            args=(self.server_args,),
        )
        proc.start()
        self.pid = proc.pid

        # Before python program terminates, call shutdown implicitly. Therefore, users don't have to explicitly call .shutdown()
        atexit.register(self.shutdown)

        # Wait for server to be ready by polling /health_generate
        start_time = time.time()
        with requests.Session() as session:
            while time.time() - start_time < launch_timeout:
                try:
                    response = session.get(f"{self.url}/health_generate")
                    if response.status_code == 200:
                        break
                except requests.RequestException:
                    pass

                if not proc.is_alive():
                    self.shutdown()
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        "Initialization failed. Please see the error messages above."
                    )

                time.sleep(2)
            else:
                self.shutdown()
                raise TimeoutError("Server failed to start within the timeout period.")

        self.endpoint = RuntimeEndpoint(self.url)

    def shutdown(self):
        from sglang.srt.utils import kill_process_tree

        if self.pid is not None:
            kill_process_tree(self.pid)
            self.pid = None

    def start_profile(self):
        self.endpoint.start_profile()

    def stop_profile(self):
        self.endpoint.stop_profile()

    def cache_prefix(self, prefix: str):
        self.endpoint.cache_prefix(prefix)

    def get_tokenizer(self):
        from sglang.srt.utils.hf_transformers_utils import get_tokenizer

        return get_tokenizer(
            self.server_args.tokenizer_path,
            tokenizer_mode=self.server_args.tokenizer_mode,
            trust_remote_code=self.server_args.trust_remote_code,
            revision=self.server_args.revision,
        )

    async def async_generate(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        sampling_params: Optional[Dict] = None,
    ):
        if self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
            json_data = {
                "input_ids": prompt,
                "sampling_params": sampling_params,
                "stream": True,
            }
        else:
            json_data = {
                "text": prompt,
                "sampling_params": sampling_params,
                "stream": True,
            }
        pos = 0

        timeout = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=3 * 3600)
        async with aiohttp.ClientSession(timeout=timeout, trust_env=True) as session:
            async with session.post(self.generate_url, json=json_data) as response:
                async for chunk, _ in response.content.iter_chunks():
                    chunk = chunk.decode("utf-8")
                    if chunk and chunk.startswith("data:"):
                        if chunk == "data: [DONE]\n\n":
                            break
                        data = json.loads(chunk[5:].strip("\n"))
                        if "text" in data:
                            cur = data["text"][pos:]
                            if cur:
                                yield cur
                            pos += len(cur)
                        else:
                            yield data

    add_request = async_generate

    def generate(
        self,
        prompt: Union[str, List[str]],
        sampling_params: Optional[Dict] = None,
        return_logprob: Optional[Union[List[bool], bool]] = False,
        logprob_start_len: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None,
        top_logprobs_num: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None,
        lora_path: Optional[List[Optional[str]]] = None,
    ):
        json_data = {
            "text": prompt,
            "sampling_params": sampling_params,
            "return_logprob": return_logprob,
            "logprob_start_len": logprob_start_len,
            "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
            "lora_path": lora_path,
        }
        assert not isinstance(lora_path, list) or len(lora_path) == len(prompt)
        response = requests.post(
            self.url + "/generate",
            json=json_data,
        )
        return json.dumps(response.json())

    def encode(
        self,
        prompt: Union[str, List[str], List[Dict], List[List[Dict]]],
    ):
        json_data = {"text": prompt}
        response = requests.post(self.url + "/encode", json=json_data)
        return json.dumps(response.json())

    async def get_server_info(self):
        async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
            async with session.get(f"{self.url}/get_server_info") as response:
                if response.status == 200:
                    return await response.json()
                else:
                    error_data = await response.json()
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        f"Failed to get server info. {error_data['error']['message']}"
                    )

    def __del__(self):
        self.shutdown()

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me also check the OpenAI-compatible API endpoint to see if there's a place where `json_schema` is handled in HTTP request processing, since the `json_path` constraint needs to be handled there too.

Let me also check the outlines backend more closely to understand how `dispatch_json` works, and look at how this would map to a `json_path` approach.

The key insight is: `json_path` is NOT a grammar-based constraint like `json_schema` or `regex`. Instead, it's a **post-generation validation** constraint. JSON path patterns like `$.store.book[*].author` specify that the generated JSON must contain certain paths - but they don't constrain the token-by-token generation process.
 
I'm reconsidering the implementation strategy. Rather than just validating the output after generation, I could convert the JSON path pattern into a JSON schema that enforces the presence of those paths, then leverage the existing `json_schema` constraint to actually guide the decoding process.

The challenge is that converting JSONPath expressions to schemas isn't straightforward, but I could add `json_path` as a new parameter throughout the pipeline and handle the conversion at the constraint processing level. For a path like `$.store.book[*].author`, I'd need to generate a schema that requires the nested structure with those specific keys and array types.

Now I'm mapping out where this constraint needs to flow through the codebase—starting from the user-facing API, through the intermediate representation layers, down to the sampling parameters and constraint handlers.

My strategy is to add `json_path` as a native parameter that gets converted to a JSON schema at the grammar backend level, which means all three existing backends automatically inherit support for it without needing individual modifications.

Let me trace through the interpreter to understand how constraints propagate through the system.

            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[stop_pos:]
        else:
            raise ValueError("Wrong type of stop in sampling parameters.")

        return comp, meta_info

    def _execute_gen(self, expr: SglGen):
        sampling_params = self._resolve_sampling_params(expr.sampling_params)
        name = expr.name
        if not self.stream:
            if self.num_api_spec_tokens is None:
                comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
                    self,
                    sampling_params=sampling_params,
                )

            else:
                if self.backend.is_chat_model:
                    # Speculative execution on models with only chat interface.
                    # Store the calls into a temporary list.
                    # They will be lazily executed later.
                    comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
                        self,
                        sampling_params=sampling_params,
                        spec_var_name=name,
                    )
                    return

                else:  # Speculative execution on models with completion interface
                    comp, meta_info = self._spec_gen(sampling_params)
            if isinstance(comp, list):
                self.text_ += comp[0]
            else:
                assert isinstance(comp, str)
                self.text_ += comp

            self.variables[name] = comp
            self.meta_info[name] = meta_info
            self.variable_event[name].set()
        else:
            assert (
                self.num_api_spec_tokens is None
            ), "stream is not supported with api speculative execution"
            generator = self.backend.generate_stream(
                self, sampling_params=sampling_params
            )

            self.variables[name] = ""
            self.stream_var_event[name].set()

            for comp, meta_info in generator:
                self.text_ += comp
                self.variables[name] += comp
                self.meta_info[name] = meta_info
                self.stream_var_event[name].set()
                self.stream_text_event.set()

            self.variable_event[name].set()
            self.stream_var_event[name].set()

    def _execute_select(self, expr: SglSelect):
        choices_decision = self.backend.select(
            self, expr.choices, expr.temperature, expr.choices_method
        )
        if expr.name is not None:
            name = expr.name
            self.variables[name] = choices_decision.decision
            self.meta_info[name] = choices_decision.meta_info
            self.variable_event[name].set()
            if self.stream_var_event:
                self.stream_var_event[name].set()
        self.text_ += choices_decision.decision

    def _execute_variable(self, expr: SglVariable):
        src_executor = expr.source_stream_executor
        value = src_executor.get_var(expr.name)
        self._execute_fill(value)

"""Pydantic models for OpenAI API protocol"""

import logging
import time
import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any, Dict, List, NamedTuple, Optional, Tuple, TypeAlias, Union

from openai.types.responses import (
    ResponseFunctionToolCall,
    ResponseInputItemParam,
    ResponseOutputItem,
    ResponseOutputMessage,
    ResponseOutputText,
    ResponseReasoningItem,
)
from openai.types.responses.response import ToolChoice
from openai.types.responses.tool import Tool
from pydantic import (
    BaseModel,
    Field,
    field_validator,
    model_serializer,
    model_validator,
)
from typing_extensions import Literal

try:
    from xgrammar import StructuralTag
except:
    StructuralTag = Any

from sglang.utils import convert_json_schema_to_str

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME = "default"


class ModelCard(BaseModel):
    """Model cards."""

    id: str
    object: str = "model"
    created: int = Field(default_factory=lambda: int(time.time()))
    owned_by: str = "sglang"
    root: Optional[str] = None
    parent: Optional[str] = None
    max_model_len: Optional[int] = None


class ModelList(BaseModel):
    """Model list consists of model cards."""

    object: str = "list"
    data: List[ModelCard] = Field(default_factory=list)


class ErrorResponse(BaseModel):
    object: str = "error"
    message: str
    type: str
    param: Optional[str] = None
    code: int


class LogProbs(BaseModel):
    text_offset: List[int] = Field(default_factory=list)
    token_logprobs: List[Optional[float]] = Field(default_factory=list)
    tokens: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    top_logprobs: List[Optional[Dict[str, float]]] = Field(default_factory=list)


class TopLogprob(BaseModel):
    token: str
    bytes: List[int]
    logprob: float


class ChatCompletionTokenLogprob(BaseModel):
    token: str
    bytes: List[int]
    logprob: float
    top_logprobs: List[TopLogprob]


class ChoiceLogprobs(BaseModel):

file.content:
class StreamOptions(BaseModel):
    include_usage: Optional[bool] = False
    continuous_usage_stats: Optional[bool] = False


class JsonSchemaResponseFormat(BaseModel):
    name: str
    description: Optional[str] = None
    # use alias to workaround pydantic conflict
    schema_: Optional[Dict[str, object]] = Field(alias="schema", default=None)
    strict: Optional[bool] = False


class ResponseFormat(BaseModel):
    type: Literal["text", "json_object", "json_schema"]
    json_schema: Optional[JsonSchemaResponseFormat] = None


class StructuresResponseFormat(BaseModel):
    begin: str
    schema_: Optional[Dict[str, object]] = Field(alias="schema", default=None)
    end: str


# NOTE(dark): keep this for backward compatibility
class LegacyStructuralTagResponseFormat(BaseModel):
    type: Literal["structural_tag"]
    structures: List[StructuresResponseFormat]
    triggers: List[str]


StructuralTagResponseFormat: TypeAlias = Union[
    LegacyStructuralTagResponseFormat, StructuralTag
]

ToolCallConstraint: TypeAlias = Union[
    Tuple[Literal["structural_tag"], StructuralTagResponseFormat],
    Tuple[Literal["json_schema"], Any],  # json_schema can be dict/str/None
]


class FileRequest(BaseModel):
    # https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/files/create
    file: bytes  # The File object (not file name) to be uploaded
    purpose: str = (
        "batch"  # The intended purpose of the uploaded file, default is "batch"
    )


class FileResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    object: str = "file"
    bytes: int
    created_at: int
    filename: str
    purpose: str


class FileDeleteResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    object: str = "file"
    deleted: bool


class BatchRequest(BaseModel):
    input_file_id: (
        str  # The ID of an uploaded file that contains requests for the new batch
    )
    endpoint: str  # The endpoint to be used for all requests in the batch
    completion_window: str  # The time frame within which the batch should be processed
    metadata: Optional[dict] = None  # Optional custom metadata for the batch


class BatchResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    object: str = "batch"
    endpoint: str
    errors: Optional[dict] = None
    input_file_id: str
    completion_window: str
    status: str = "validating"
    output_file_id: Optional[str] = None
    error_file_id: Optional[str] = None
    created_at: int
    in_progress_at: Optional[int] = None
    expires_at: Optional[int] = None
    finalizing_at: Optional[int] = None
    completed_at: Optional[int] = None
    failed_at: Optional[int] = None
    expired_at: Optional[int] = None
    cancelling_at: Optional[int] = None
    cancelled_at: Optional[int] = None
    request_counts: Optional[dict] = None
    metadata: Optional[dict] = None


class CompletionRequest(BaseModel):
    # Ordered by official OpenAI API documentation
    # https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create
    model: str = Field(
        default=DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME,
        description="Model name. Supports LoRA adapters via 'base-model:adapter-name' syntax.",
    )
    prompt: Union[List[int], List[List[int]], str, List[str]]
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    echo: bool = False
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    max_tokens: int = 16
    n: int = 1
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None
    stream: bool = False
    stream_options: Optional[StreamOptions] = None
    suffix: Optional[str] = None
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    user: Optional[str] = None
    return_hidden_states: bool = False
    return_routed_experts: bool = False
    return_cached_tokens_details: bool = False

    # Extra parameters for SRT backend only and will be ignored by OpenAI models.
    top_k: int = -1
    min_p: float = 0.0
    min_tokens: int = 0
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
    regex: Optional[str] = None
    ebnf: Optional[str] = None
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None
    no_stop_trim: bool = False
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    lora_path: Optional[Union[List[Optional[str]], Optional[str]]] = None
    session_params: Optional[Dict] = None
    response_format: Optional[Union[ResponseFormat, StructuralTagResponseFormat]] = None

640	    @classmethod
641	    def normalize_reasoning_inputs(cls, values: Dict):
642	        r = values.get("reasoning")
643	        if r is None:
644	            return values
645	
646	        if isinstance(r, dict):
647	            effort = r.get("effort") or r.get("reasoning_effort")
648	            if effort in {"low", "medium", "high"}:
649	                values["reasoning_effort"] = effort
650	
651	            enabled = (
652	                r.get("enabled")
653	                if r.get("enabled") is not None
654	                else r.get("enable", False)
655	            )
656	            if isinstance(enabled, str):
657	                enabled = enabled.strip().lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "y", "on"}
658	            if enabled:
659	                ctk = values.get("chat_template_kwargs")
660	                if not isinstance(ctk, dict):
661	                    ctk = {}
662	                ctk.setdefault("thinking", True)
663	                values["chat_template_kwargs"] = ctk
664	
665	        return values

667	    @model_validator(mode="before")
668	    @classmethod
669	    def set_json_schema(cls, values):
670	        response_format = values.get("response_format")
671	        if not response_format:
672	            return values
673	
674	        if response_format.get("type") != "json_schema":
675	            return values
676	
677	        schema = response_format.pop("schema", None)
678	        json_schema = response_format.get("json_schema")
679	
680	        if json_schema:
681	            return values
682	
683	        if schema:
684	            name_ = schema.get("title", "Schema")
685	            strict_ = False
686	            if "properties" in schema and "strict" in schema["properties"]:
687	                item = schema["properties"].pop("strict", None)
688	                if item and item.get("default", False):
689	                    strict_ = True
690	
691	            response_format["json_schema"] = {
692	                "name": name_,
693	                "schema": schema,
694	                "strict": strict_,
695	            }
696	
697	        return values

699	    def to_sampling_params(
700	        self,
701	        stop: List[str],
702	        model_generation_config: Dict[str, Any],
703	        tool_call_constraint: Optional[ToolCallConstraint] = None,
704	    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
705	        """
706	        Convert request to sampling parameters.
707	        Priority: user value > model generation_config > OpenAI defaults
708	        """
709	
710	        def get_param(param_name: str):
711	            value = getattr(self, param_name)
712	            if value is None:
713	                return model_generation_config.get(
714	                    param_name, self._DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS[param_name]
715	                )
716	            return value
717	
718	        # add per user request
719	        spaces_between_special_tokens = (
720	            True
721	            if self.chat_template_kwargs is None
722	            else self.chat_template_kwargs.get("spaces_between_special_tokens", True)
723	        )
724	
725	        sampling_params = {
726	            "temperature": get_param("temperature"),
727	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_completion_tokens or self.max_tokens,
728	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_tokens,
729	            "stop": stop,
730	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
731	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
732	            "top_p": get_param("top_p"),
733	            "top_k": get_param("top_k"),
734	            "min_p": get_param("min_p"),
735	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
736	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
737	            "repetition_penalty": get_param("repetition_penalty"),
738	            "regex": self.regex,
739	            "ebnf": self.ebnf,
740	            "n": self.n,
741	            "no_stop_trim": self.no_stop_trim,
742	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
743	            "skip_special_tokens": self.skip_special_tokens,
744	            "logit_bias": self.logit_bias,
745	            "custom_params": self.custom_params,
746	            "sampling_seed": self.seed,
747	            "spaces_between_special_tokens": spaces_between_special_tokens,
748	        }
749	
750	        if self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_schema":
751	            sampling_params["json_schema"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
752	                self.response_format.json_schema.schema_
753	            )
754	        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "json_object":
755	            sampling_params["json_schema"] = '{"type": "object"}'
756	        elif self.response_format and self.response_format.type == "structural_tag":
757	            sampling_params["structural_tag"] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
758	                self.response_format.model_dump(by_alias=True)
759	            )
760	
761	        # Check if there are already existing output constraints
762	        has_existing_constraints = (
763	            sampling_params.get("regex")
764	            or sampling_params.get("ebnf")
765	            or sampling_params.get("structural_tag")
766	            or sampling_params.get("json_schema")
767	        )
768	
769	        if tool_call_constraint and has_existing_constraints:
770	            logger.warning("Constrained decoding is not compatible with tool calls.")
771	        elif tool_call_constraint:
772	            constraint_type, constraint_value = tool_call_constraint
773	            if constraint_type == "structural_tag":
774	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
775	                    constraint_value.model_dump(by_alias=True)
776	                )
777	            elif constraint_type == "json_schema":
778	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = convert_json_schema_to_str(
779	                    constraint_value  # type: ignore
780	                )
781	            else:
782	                sampling_params[constraint_type] = constraint_value
783	
784	        return sampling_params

custom_params: Optional[Dict] = None
    custom_logit_processor: Optional[str] = None

    # For PD disaggregation
    bootstrap_host: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    bootstrap_port: Optional[Union[List[Optional[int]], int]] = None
    bootstrap_room: Optional[Union[List[int], int]] = None

    # For data parallel rank routing
    data_parallel_rank: Optional[int] = None

    # For request id
    rid: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    # Extra key for classifying the request (e.g. cache_salt)
    extra_key: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    # Cache salt for request caching
    cache_salt: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
    # Priority for the request
    priority: Optional[int] = None

    # For custom metric labels
    custom_labels: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None

    @field_validator("max_tokens")
    @classmethod
    def validate_max_tokens_positive(cls, v):
        if v is not None and v <= 0:
            raise ValueError("max_tokens must be positive")
        return v


class SglExt(BaseModel):
    """SGLang extension fields for OpenAI-compatible responses.

    Future SGLang-specific extensions to OpenAI-compatible response objects
    should be added as fields here rather than directly on the choice object.
    """

    routed_experts: Optional[str] = None
    cached_tokens_details: Optional[CachedTokensDetails] = None

    @model_serializer(mode="wrap")
    def _serialize(self, handler):
        data = handler(self)
        # Remove None fields to keep response clean
        return {k: v for k, v in data.items() if v is not None}


class CompletionResponseChoice(BaseModel):
    index: int
    text: str
    logprobs: Optional[LogProbs] = None
    finish_reason: Optional[Literal["stop", "length", "content_filter", "abort"]] = None
    matched_stop: Union[None, int, str] = None
    hidden_states: Optional[object] = None

    @model_serializer(mode="wrap")
    def _serialize(self, handler):
        data = handler(self)
        if self.hidden_states is None:
            data.pop("hidden_states", None)
        return data


class CompletionResponse(BaseModel):
    id: str
    object: str = "text_completion"
    created: int = Field(default_factory=lambda: int(time.time()))
    model: str
    choices: List[CompletionResponseChoice]
    usage: UsageInfo
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
    sglext: Optional[SglExt] = None

    @model_serializer(mode="wrap")
    def _serialize(self, handler):
        data = handler(self)
        if self.sglext is None:
            data.pop("sglext", None)
        return data


class CompletionResponseStreamChoice(BaseModel):
    index: int
    text: str
    logprobs: Optional[LogProbs] = None
    finish_reason: Optional[Literal["stop", "length", "content_filter", "abort"]] = None
    matched_stop: Union[None, int, str] = None
    hidden_states: Optional[object] = None

    @model_serializer(mode="wrap")
    def _serialize(self, handler):
        data = handler(self)
        if self.hidden_states is None:
            data.pop("hidden_states", None)
        return data

    id: str

import unittest

from sglang.srt.utils import kill_process_tree
from sglang.test.ci.ci_register import register_amd_ci, register_cuda_ci
from sglang.test.kits.ebnf_constrained_kit import TestEBNFConstrainedMixin
from sglang.test.kits.json_constrained_kit import TestJSONConstrainedMixin
from sglang.test.kits.regex_constrained_kit import TestRegexConstrainedMixin
from sglang.test.test_utils import (
    DEFAULT_SMALL_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST,
    DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_FOR_SERVER_LAUNCH,
    DEFAULT_URL_FOR_TEST,
    CustomTestCase,
    popen_launch_server,
)

register_cuda_ci(est_time=111, suite="stage-b-test-small-1-gpu")
register_amd_ci(est_time=179, suite="stage-b-test-small-1-gpu-amd")


class ServerWithGrammar(CustomTestCase):
    backend = "xgrammar"
    disable_overlap = False

    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        cls.model = DEFAULT_SMALL_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST
        cls.base_url = DEFAULT_URL_FOR_TEST
        launch_args = [
            "--max-running-requests",
            "10",
            "--grammar-backend",
            cls.backend,
        ]

        if cls.disable_overlap:
            launch_args += ["--disable-overlap-schedule"]

        cls.process = popen_launch_server(
            cls.model,
            cls.base_url,
            timeout=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_FOR_SERVER_LAUNCH,
            other_args=launch_args,
        )

    @classmethod
    def tearDownClass(cls):
        kill_process_tree(cls.process.pid)


class TestXGrammarBackend(
    ServerWithGrammar,
    TestJSONConstrainedMixin,
    TestEBNFConstrainedMixin,
    TestRegexConstrainedMixin,
):
    backend = "xgrammar"


class TestOutlinesBackend(ServerWithGrammar, TestJSONConstrainedMixin):
    backend = "outlines"


class TestLLGuidanceBackend(
    ServerWithGrammar,
    TestJSONConstrainedMixin,
    TestEBNFConstrainedMixin,
    TestRegexConstrainedMixin,
):
    backend = "llguidance"


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

input.prompt:
I need to design a plan for adding a new `json_path` output constraint to SGLang that validates generated output is valid JSON containing a specified JSON path pattern (e.g., `$.store.book[*].author`).

## How the existing constraint system works

SGLang has a layered architecture for constrained decoding:

### 1. User API (`python/sglang/lang/api.py`)
The `gen()` function accepts constraint params like `regex`, `json_schema`, `dtype`. These create `SglGen` IR nodes.

### 2. IR layer (`python/sglang/lang/ir.py`)
`SglSamplingParams` dataclass holds `dtype`, `regex`, `json_schema` fields. `SglGen.__init__()` takes all these and stores them in a `SglSamplingParams`.

### 3. Backend layer (`python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`)
`_handle_dtype_to_regex()` converts dtype to regex. `to_srt_kwargs()` in `SglSamplingParams` serializes `regex` and `json_schema` for the server request.

### 4. Server SamplingParams (`python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`)
`SamplingParams.__init__()` accepts `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf`, `structural_tag`. It enforces mutual exclusivity: only one of regex/json_schema/ebnf can be set.

### 5. Grammar Manager (`python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`)
`process_req_with_grammar()` checks which constraint is set, creates a key tuple like `("json", schema_string)`, and dispatches to the grammar backend.

### 6. Base Grammar Backend (`python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`)
`_init_value_dispatch()` routes by key type: "json" → `dispatch_json()`, "regex" → `dispatch_regex()`, etc. Each backend (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance) implements these dispatch methods.

### 7. OpenAI-compatible API (`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`)
`CompletionRequest` and `ChatCompletionRequest` have `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf` fields. `to_sampling_params()` maps response_format → sampling_params.

### 8. gRPC server (`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/grpc_server.py`)
Also handles `json_schema`, `regex`, `ebnf_grammar` fields.

## Design approach

The `json_path` constraint should work by converting a JSONPath expression to a JSON schema, then delegating to the existing `json_schema` constrained decoding infrastructure. This way all existing backends (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance) automatically support it.

For example, `$.store.book[*].author` should produce a JSON schema like:
```json
{
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["store"],
  "properties": {
    "store": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["book"],
      "properties": {
        "book": {
          "type": "array",
          "items": {
            "type": "object",
            "required": ["author"],
            "properties": {
              "author": {}
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Files to modify

1. **`python/sglang/lang/api.py`** - Add `json_path` param to `gen()`
2. **`python/sglang/lang/ir.py`** - Add `json_path` field to `SglSamplingParams` and `SglGen`
3. **`python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`** - Add `json_path` field, include in mutual exclusivity check
4. **`python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py`** - Add `dispatch_json_path()` method that converts JSONPath → JSON schema → delegates to `dispatch_json()`
5. **`python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`** - Add handling for `json_path` in `process_req_with_grammar()`
6. **`python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`** - Add `json_path` field to `CompletionRequest` and `ChatCompletionRequest`
7. **New file: `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_utils.py`** - JSONPath → JSON Schema converter

## Questions for the plan

1. Should the JSONPath → JSON schema conversion happen in the grammar backend (`dispatch_json_path`), or earlier in the pipeline (e.g., in the IR layer)?
2. The conversion utility needs to parse JSONPath expressions. Should we implement a minimal parser ourselves (no external dependency) or use a library? Given the user said "Do NOT try to run or install SGLang", a self-contained implementation makes sense.
3. What JSONPath features to support? At minimum: `$` (root), `.property` (child), `[*]` (array wildcard), `..property` (recursive descent).

Please design a detailed implementation plan covering all files, the JSONPath parser, the JSON schema converter, and how it integrates with the existing constraint system.

59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197	
198	# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
201	# token
202	def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
203	    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))
204	
205	
206	MAX_LEN = 2**30
207	
208	
209	def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
210	    total = 0
211	    for token, value in subpattern:
212	        if token in {
213	            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
214	            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
215	            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
216	        }:
217	            total += 1
218	        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
219	            # EG: (a\d+) ->
220	            # [(SUBPATTERN,
221	            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
222	            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
223	            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
224	            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
225	        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
226	            _, branches = value
227	            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
228	        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
229	            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
230	            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
231	                total += MAX_LEN
232	            else:
233	                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
234	        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
235	            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
236	            # length
237	            total += 0
238	        else:
239	            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")
240	
241	            total += MAX_LEN
242	
243	    return total

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )
222	
223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )
303	
304	    def trace(self, *, backend=None, **kwargs):
305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)
309	
310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program
312	
313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
314	        return cache_program(self, backend)
315	
316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]
395

397	class SglExprList(SglExpr):
398	    def __init__(self, expr_list: List[SglExpr]):
399	        super().__init__()
400	        self.expr_list = expr_list
401	
402	    def __repr__(self):
403	        return f"ExprList({self.expr_list})"

406	class SglArgument(SglExpr):
407	    def __init__(self, name: str, value: str):
408	        super().__init__()
409	        self.name = name
410	        self.value = value

619	class SglSeparateReasoning(SglExpr):
620	    def __init__(self, model_type: str, expr: SglExpr):
621	        super().__init__()
622	        self.model_type = model_type
623	
624	        self.expr = expr
625	        self.name = None
626	        self._process_expr(expr)
627	
628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"

2	
3	import logging
4	import time
5	from concurrent import futures
6	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List
7	
8	import torch
9	
10	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
11	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
12	    create_grammar_backend,
13	)
14	from sglang.srt.environ import envs
15	
16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	
24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs

42	class BaseGrammarObject:
43	
44	    def __init__(self):
45	        self._finished = False
46	        self.grammar_stats = None
47	        self.current_token = None
48	
49	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
50	        pass
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
53	        """
54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()
57	
58	    def rollback(self, k: int):
59	        raise NotImplementedError()
60	
61	    def is_terminated(self):
62	        return False
63	
64	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
65	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
66	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
70	        raise NotImplementedError()
71	
72	    @staticmethod
73	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
74	        raise NotImplementedError()
75	
76	    @staticmethod
77	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
78	        raise NotImplementedError()
79	
80	    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
81	        return self
82	
83	    @property
84	    def finished(self):
85	        return self._finished
86	
87	    @finished.setter
88	    def finished(self, finished):
89	        self._finished = finished
90	
91	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
92	        """
93	        Try to jump forward in the grammar.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            A jump forward helper which may be used in `jump_forward_str_state`.
97	            None if the jump forward is not possible.
98	        """
99	        raise NotImplementedError()
100	
101	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
102	        """
103	        Jump forward for the grammar.
104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """
109	        raise NotImplementedError()
110	
111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:
114	        """
115	        Jump forward occurs, and update the grammar state if needed.
116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError()

120	INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ = BaseGrammarObject()

123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197
